noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of Gallagher's data organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits Gallagher best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits Gallagher's agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: The AI program at Gallagher sits inside an acquisitive, regulated professional-services business with thousands of producers, service teams and carrier relationships. In that setting, a frontier model has to be useful across knowledge work and software delivery without creating an unmanageable decision surface. Anthropic gives the data organization one platform for reasoning over client exposures, policy documents, claims histories, benefit plans, carrier terms and service correspondence, orchestrating bounded agents and assisting development across brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications. Claude's focus on steerability and predictable behavior is especially relevant where confidential client data, regulatory variation, hallucination risk and accountable advice cannot be bolted on later. The result would be a safer path from isolated copilots to consistent service and insight across acquired businesses without slowing integration.
cases: Reason across client exposures, policy documents, claims histories, benefit plans, carrier terms and service correspondence without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: The information gap at Gallagher is not always a missing database; often it is what producers, account teams, claims professionals, consultants and integration leaders know but do not record during client meetings, renewal discussions, claims interviews and field-service notes. Wispr Flow lets users speak naturally into existing desktop and mobile tools, producing structured, edited text with commands and filler-word cleanup. That makes it well suited to distributed or frontline work around quote-to-bind, renewal, claims and acquisition-integration workflows. The data organization gains more timely operational context without asking teams to adopt a separate note-taking system or spend the end of a shift reconstructing what happened.
cases: Turn client meetings, renewal discussions, claims interviews and field-service notes into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: Gallagher is an acquisitive, regulated professional-services business with thousands of producers, service teams and carrier relationships, but many critical interactions still happen through web interfaces rather than clean service integrations. Browserbase gives the technology team an infrastructure layer for agents to use carrier portals, client sites, acquisition-era tools, benefits systems and regulatory portals with controlled sessions and repeatable execution. It can also parallelize browser testing for brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications, catching failures before they reach producers, account teams, claims professionals, consultants and integration leaders. That makes it a useful bridge between near-term automation and longer-term modernization by removing small web-based bottlenecks that stand in the way of consistent service and insight across acquired businesses without slowing integration.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across carrier portals, client sites, acquisition-era tools, benefits systems and regulatory portals without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: Gallagher's engineering surface spans brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications, with different dependencies, access requirements and release cadences. Coder gives the data organization a way to define those environments once and deliver them as secure cloud workspaces. Developers gain a ready-to-use stack; security teams keep code and sensitive tooling away from unmanaged endpoints; platform teams can observe utilization and control compute. That creates a practical foundation for consistent service and insight across acquired businesses without slowing integration, particularly as AI-assisted development increases both the pace of change and the need for reproducible environments.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: As Gallagher adds agents to quote-to-bind, renewal, claims and acquisition-integration workflows, every useful workflow introduces a non-human principal with credentials, permissions and an owner—or an ownership gap. Token Security matters because it treats those identities as a distinct governance domain rather than an extension of employee access. The platform can continuously find integration accounts, service bots, APIs and producer or claims agents, govern their lifecycle and enforce access based on intended purpose. That would help Gallagher scale automation while keeping the blast radius around client, policy, carrier, claims, benefits and acquisition data narrow and the audit trail clear.
cases: Inventory integration accounts, service bots, APIs and producer or claims agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific client, policy, carrier, claims, benefits and acquisition data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: Gallagher has practical multimodal needs across risk survey imagery, claims documentation and client education content. fal matters because it gives product and engineering teams a high-speed inference layer for image, video, audio and voice models through simple APIs, without forcing each use case to become an infrastructure project. The data organization could use it to prototype and scale bounded generative experiences alongside brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications, from content production to simulation or visual assistance. The strongest fit is where latency and iteration speed matter but the risk profile around confidential client data, regulatory variation, hallucination risk and accountable advice still requires approved models, controlled inputs and a clear review path.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new producer, account-service, claims and client-facing capabilities.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: Gallagher is an acquisitive, regulated professional-services business with thousands of producers, service teams and carrier relationships, so a small defect in client, policy, carrier, claims, benefits and acquisition data can propagate across many users and workflows. Monte Carlo matters because it extends data observability into the model and agent layer rather than treating each as a separate monitoring stack. The technology team could trace an unreliable output back through pipelines, transformations and code, then prioritize remediation by downstream consequence. This is particularly relevant to quote-to-bind, renewal, claims and acquisition-integration workflows, where the cost of a silent issue is measured in service, safety, revenue or trust—not only in a failed job.
cases: Monitor client, policy, carrier, claims, benefits and acquisition data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining consistent service and insight across acquired businesses without slowing integration.

tonic.pull: The teams building brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications need realistic data, but using production copies of client, employee-benefit, policy, claims and acquisition data creates unnecessary exposure and slows access approvals. Tonic matters because it can generate synthetic, de-identified datasets that preserve the shape and utility of enterprise data for development, testing and AI work. For Gallagher, that would let engineers reproduce edge cases, train or evaluate models and refresh lower environments without moving identifiable records into places they do not belong. The result is faster delivery with fewer sensitive records outside production—important in an environment shaped by confidential client data, regulatory variation, hallucination risk and accountable advice.
cases: Replace production copies of client, employee-benefit, policy, claims and acquisition data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: Gallagher depends on global offices, acquired environments, cloud platforms and partner-connected networks, but automation is only as reliable as the inventory and configuration data behind it. NetBox Labs matters as a source of truth for documenting, managing and automating IT and network infrastructure. It could give the data organization one current model of sites, devices, circuits, addresses and dependencies across that estate. That foundation makes changes to hybrid and multi-cloud workloads inherited across a growing brokerage platform safer, improves incident context and gives infrastructure agents a trusted state to read before they act. It replaces stale spreadsheets with governed infrastructure context and supports consistent service and insight across acquired businesses without slowing integration.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across global offices, acquired environments, cloud platforms and partner-connected networks.
Drive provisioning and change validation for hybrid and multi-cloud workloads inherited across a growing brokerage platform from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: For Gallagher, the highest-value AI workloads sit close to client, policy, carrier, claims, benefits and acquisition data and often need bursty batch or agentic execution. Valar provides an in-VPC inference platform built for that pattern, using scheduling, routing and accelerator placement to turn installed compute into more usable capacity. It would give platform teams one operating layer for renewal preparation, policy comparison, claims support and client-service agents, while prompts, outputs and telemetry remain within the enterprise boundary. That makes Valar a strong fit where external model traffic is constrained by confidential client data, regulatory variation, hallucination risk and accountable advice, but the business still needs production-grade throughput.
cases: Run renewal preparation, policy comparison, claims support and client-service agents inside Gallagher's own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: As Gallagher increases the release cadence across brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications, the organization needs secure dependencies that move at the same speed. Echo's model is to patch and harden the underlying components before teams consume them, rather than asking every application owner to remediate the same CVEs independently. This could materially simplify the software supply chain for brokerage, claims, benefits, integration and analytics applications, including agent and AI workloads. For the data organization, the benefit is fewer inherited vulnerabilities, more consistent provenance and less conflict between developer velocity and the controls needed in an environment shaped by confidential client data, regulatory variation, hallucination risk and accountable advice.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across brokerage, claims, benefits, integration and analytics applications.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: The cloud-security question for Gallagher is not only who has access; it is which application, service or agent can communicate with which other system. Act Security is built to answer that question continuously. By learning intended and observed pathways across hybrid and multi-cloud workloads inherited across a growing brokerage platform, the platform can identify unnecessary reachability and help teams lock it down with business context intact. That supports consistent service and insight across acquired businesses without slowing integration by reducing lateral movement and configuration ambiguity while preserving the connections its people and production workflows genuinely need.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across hybrid and multi-cloud workloads inherited across a growing brokerage platform.
Create least-privilege perimeters around brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: The security risk in Gallagher's roadmap is often created before code exists—when teams define new producer, account-service, claims and client-facing capabilities without the right control requirements. Clover Security is built for that point in the lifecycle. By reading planning artifacts and returning actionable guidance inside existing product workflows, it can connect security leaders with the teams building brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications. For the data organization, the value is earlier visibility, clearer ownership and less rework, especially because late vulnerabilities or design corrections are disproportionately costly in an environment shaped by confidential client data, regulatory variation, hallucination risk and accountable advice.
cases: Review new producer, account-service, claims and client-facing capabilities while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: Gallagher's current agenda—integrating a larger global brokerage footprint while standardizing systems, analytics and practical AI across brokerage, benefits and claims operations—means trust has to keep pace with releases, integrations and agent adoption. Drata's platform can continuously monitor the control environment around brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications, organize evidence and connect risk to accountable owners. That helps the data organization move away from spreadsheet-driven point-in-time compliance toward an operating model in which readiness is always visible. For an organization exposed to confidential client data, regulatory variation, hallucination risk and accountable advice, that also improves the speed and consistency of customer, regulator and partner assurance.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across brokerage, benefits, claims, analytics and client-service applications and hybrid and multi-cloud workloads inherited across a growing brokerage platform.
Reuse assurance work across insurance and benefits regulation, privacy, audit, model governance and third-party risk instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: Cloud and AI assets at Gallagher will change faster as the organization pursues its current agenda: integrating a larger global brokerage footprint while standardizing systems, analytics and practical AI across brokerage, benefits and claims operations. Orca Security discovers that estate agentlessly, maps the relationships among workloads, identities, data and vulnerabilities, and ranks risk by likely impact. Applied to hybrid and multi-cloud workloads inherited across a growing brokerage platform, it can give security and platform teams a shared picture of the exposures affecting client, policy, carrier, claims, benefits and acquisition data. The result is fewer blind spots, less alert fatigue and a remediation plan grounded in actual attack paths—all in service of consistent service and insight across acquired businesses without slowing integration.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across Gallagher's cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive Gallagher data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
